description_long: This object consists of a dark wooden expandable accordion-style wall rack with multiple pegs, from which a single white towel is hanging. The rack features a crisscross lattice design, with four diamond-shaped sections and six pegs protruding outward for hanging items. The towel is draped over one of the central pegs, hanging down naturally and showing a slight border detail. The wood appears to have a matte finish, and the pegs are rounded for easy hanging. The towel is plain, with no visible patterns except for a subtle border. This setup is commonly found in entryways, bathrooms, or utility rooms for hanging towels, coats, or accessories.
description: A dark wooden accordion wall rack with pegs, holding a plain white towel draped over one peg. The rack has a crisscross lattice design and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the rack and the towel hanging down, with pegs extending outward.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full lattice structure, all pegs, and the towel hanging from a central peg.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again highlighting the rack's depth and the towel's drape.
description_view_3: Rear or alternate front view, showing the rack's structure and the towel's placement from a slightly different angle.
